findings.message: loadItemsQuietly() catches the fetchItems() rejection and returns [] with no rethrow and no telemetry. The RUM js-errors counter is fed exclusively by the window 'error'/'unhandledrejection' listeners in observability/rum.ts (lines 46-49); a swallowed promise rejection fires neither, so a failed item load becomes completely invisible to monitoring. The user sees the 'No items yet.' empty state, and operators see no error signal.
findings.suggestion: Do not swallow the failure. Either let it reject so the unhandledrejection listener fires, or explicitly report it to RUM before returning a fallback, e.g. catch (e) { reportRumError(e); throw e; } and surface the existing error state (setError(true)) so the failure reaches both the user and the js-errors counter.
